# Heritable Contamination Registry

## What It Deepens

This card makes legible a consequence the canon already names without institutionalizing. `MAGIC_OR_TECH_SYSTEMS.md` §Long-Term Consequences states flatly that "generational health impact in extractor families is empirical reality, only partially studied"; CF-0005 commits that artifact effects always cost and that costs include slow contamination and generational health impact; CF-0025 and its extension in `ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md` §Inequality Patterns explicitly flag a post-career occult-fallout specialist stratum "analogous to extractor-veteran families" with contamination injuries and no chartered pension. The world acknowledges the cost and leaves the welfare response to informal custom. This card fills the gap with a characteristically Charter-Era instrument — voluntary, civic-registry-mediated, polity-asymmetric — and makes the contamination-lineage stratum an institutionally visible category rather than a silent one. It also closes a partial loop with `OPEN_QUESTIONS.md` §Post-Career Contractor Pension and Stigma, offering a concrete institutional form that the open-question frame already anticipates (without foreclosing the open question, since the registry is adopted only in some polities).

## Why It Fits This World

Animalia is Charter-Era-administered and guild-mediated; its working answer to "who pays for the cost of artifact work" has always been the chartered guild in partnership with civic registry, with the cracks filled by informal mutual aid. The Incident Wave has pressurized that answer: widows of extractors, widows of the job (CF-0024), widows of the expedition (CF-0028), and the newly-named contamination-injured fallout-specialists (CF-0025) have all accumulated without a consolidated welfare mechanism. The registry is what this world would plausibly invent in response — not a universal program (that is not how this polity-asymmetric world works), but a chartered-polity experiment that sharpens existing regional asymmetries. Tonally it fits: the rite of registering marks a family for three generations, which is Animalia's kind of bureaucratic gravity — earthy, consequential, contested. The stabilizers are load-bearing: voluntary, civic-not-guild, regionally adopted, marking duration capped. None of those are decorative; each prevents the registry from collapsing into either pure welfare utopia or dystopian surveillance.

## Immediate Consequences

In the polities that adopt it (mostly larger chartered heartland cities), extractor and containment-wright households face a new domestic deliberation: register the aging patriarch with the contamination-injury mark and secure a modest pension supplement for the widow, or keep the family off the rolls and preserve marriage prospects for the grandchildren. Monastic clinics in adopting polities quietly adjust intake to give registered patients priority; barber-surgeons and herb-wives in artisan quarters learn to ask the referral question. Post-career occult-fallout specialists (CF-0025) in adopting polities gain a modest pension path that did not exist before; unregistered specialists remain under the old contract-custom scheme. Guild pension committees adjust their supplement formulas to match civic-registry disbursement schedules, and the containment-wright / broker schism inside the artifact-extraction guilds acquires a new fault-line (containment-wrights suspect the civic gaze; brokers see a market-legibility asset).

## Longer-Term Consequences

Over two generations, a visible stratum of registered versus unregistered contamination-lineage households forms in artisan quarters of adopting polities. This stratum compounds existing stigma rather than dissolving it; the mark becomes a visible social category that third-generation heirs inherit regardless of their own career choices, and inheritance disputes pick up the registry-mark as an asset-class marker. Migrating extractor families face registry-transfer gaps when they move from an adopting polity to a non-adopting one, paralleling the CF-0021 crafter-mobility and CF-0024 contractor-mobility patterns — a third axis of Charter-Era jurisdictional friction under the no-inter-polity-schema recognition gap. Principled-refuser clergy (CF-0026) take up the registry as a moral-witness issue; some faction campaigns for mandatory enrollment with stronger protections, others campaign for abolition of the three-generation mark as stigmatizing. In magistrate courts, a small but durable docket of registry-evasion and fraudulent-registration cases accumulates, and in punitive polities an underground "registry-scrubber" trade emerges. Inside the artifact-extraction guilds the registry sharpens the containment-wright / broker schism further: containment-wrights treat it as a civic-surveillance wedge, brokers as a market-legibility asset, inspectors as yet another stretched administrative burden.

## Risks

The registry sits on the Charter-Era civic-liberty line. The stabilizers (voluntary enrollment, welfare purpose, three-generation cap, civic-registry not guild) are load-bearing; if any single one is compromised — say, a polity mandates registration, or a crown's civic watch begins using the register for contractor-vetting purposes — the whole instrument tips from welfare toward surveillance, and the canon-addition adjudication would need to narrow scope or reclassify toward contested_canon. Rule 3 specialness-inflation is a live risk around the three-generation marker itself: if the mark becomes a social caste-marker rather than an administrative record, the registry has introduced a new hereditary status category into a world whose existing stigma patterns are already dense. The no-inter-polity-schema gap is a feature (matches the CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028 pattern), not a bug, but it sharpens migration-case pressure on magistrate courts. M-5 (sentience as artifact effect) is firewalled here but canon-addition should re-test — a "hereditary contamination" institution could in future be misread as evidence for heritable-Maker-touching; the registry must remain an empirical-body record without ontological claim about the body.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Moderate. `INSTITUTIONS.md` §Law/Custom/Judgment and §Trade/Guilds need registry language added; §Healing/Medicine needs the monastic-clinic-priority hook. `ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md` §Inequality Patterns gains a registered-vs-unregistered stratum note; §Wage Spreads picks up pension-supplement annotation. `EVERYDAY_LIFE.md` (a) big-city and (b) estate outskirts gain the decision-to-register household texture. `OPEN_QUESTIONS.md` §Post-Career Contractor Pension partially closes (adopting-polity branch); remaining openness carries forward. `CANON_LEDGER.md` receives the new CF record and a change-log entry; CF-0005, CF-0008, CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0025, CF-0026 gain cross-reference annotations. No invariant revision needed.

## Likely Story Yield

High and varied. Story engines this card activates: a registered widow versus an unregistered one in a succession case; a contamination-lineage young person refusing the mark and rupturing family economics; an inspector finding registry-scrubber work and facing the decision about what to report; a principled-refuser clergy member working on registry-reform and getting caught between welfare realism and abolitionist conviction; a civic watch attempting to misuse the registry for contractor-vetting and triggering a guild-civic confrontation; a migrating extractor family navigating the polity-asymmetric schema gap; a registry-fraud scandal disclosing that a named family has been scrubbed off for generations.

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon` as proposed. Regional scope is load-bearing to the world's polity-asymmetric character. Promotion to `hard_canon` would over-universalize. Demotion to `contested_canon` would under-institutionalize — the point is that some polities have solidly built this, in the same way they have solidly built civic-registry in general.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 7a (Invariants)**: All 16 invariants tested. ONT-1/2/3 respected (no sentience, magic, or interbreeding claim). CAU-1 reinforced (registry makes the cost structurally visible). CAU-2 and CAU-3 untouched. DIS-1 untouched. DIS-2 respected (civic registry already partial-literacy-gated). DIS-3 untouched. SOC-1 respected (class mobility preserved; the registry is occupational-lineage, not species-coded). SOC-3 respected (pension-supplement is coin-contractual). SOC-4 respected (artifact-trade remains guild-chartered; registry is civic-welfare-adjacent, not guild-displacing). AES-1/2/3 respected — particularly AES-2 (ordinary life keeps world honest): making the cost legible in civic text is the tonal move this world makes. No invariant breach. No invariant-revision pathway fires.

**Phase 7b (Mystery Reserve Firewall)**: All 10 MR entries checked. M-1 / M-2 / M-3 / M-4 / M-8 / M-9 / M-10: no overlap with the card's statement or consequences. M-5 (sentience as artifact effect): firewalled — the registry is a body-ledger, not an ontological claim; no sentience implication. M-6 (vessel-hosted agencies): no overlap. M-7 (specialist reputation-genealogy): touched tangentially — the registry records contamination-injury, not knowledge-claims, so the "oracle reading" vs "procedural-competence reading" dispute is untouched; a registered specialist's reputation does not become registry-authoritative. No closures in any direction. Firewall intact.

**Phase 7c (Distribution Discipline)**: CFs consulted: CF-0005 (cost required), CF-0008 (guild-licensed extraction — registry does not displace it), CF-0011 (class mobility — registry does not violate), CF-0021 (crafter mobility pattern — registry follows same polity-asymmetric logic), CF-0024 (contractor mobility pattern — same), CF-0025 (fallout-specialist stratum — the card's primary extension target), CF-0026 (principled-refuser tradition — the card's likely political interlocutor). `recommended_scope: regional / current / public` with four concrete `why_not_universal` stabilizers. No contradiction with any consulted CF's stabilizers. No silent global implication.

**Phase 7e Repairs Applied**: none fired.
